concerns.text: Task T012a and T017a implement ingestion of the 'SPICE' dataset as the primary source, whereas spec FR-001 explicitly mandates ingesting the 'ILThermo dataset and the curated SAPT/DFT energy decomposition repository'. The tasks silently replace the required data sources with an unverified alternative (SPICE), violating the functional requirement.

Spec FR-002 mandates generating 'partial charges' as part of the unified feature set. Task T016 explicitly instructs to 'DROP the partial_charge column from the final dataframe'. While the plan justifies this to avoid circular validation, the task list fails to reflect the spec's requirement to generate and include these descriptors, effectively weakening the FR without explicit spec amendment.

Spec FR-007 and SC-003 require validation against 'experimental enthalpy of mixing data' from ILThermo. Tasks T035a and T035c implement validation against a 'generated DFT dataset' (synthetic). This is a semantic weakening of the requirement; the tasks do not implement the specified experimental validation, leaving the success criterion unverified as defined in the spec.

Task T015 calculates partial charges and creates a 'charge_reliability' column, but the spec does not require 'charge_reliability' in the final output. This introduces a silent scope addition (a new data artifact) that is not anchored to any User Story or FR, violating the principle of not adding unverified artifacts.

Spec FR-006 requires an ANOVA test to determine if family membership explains variance in interaction terms (implied on model predictions or the unified dataset). Tasks T029a/b perform ANOVA on the 'raw_sapt_subset' (training data). This is a semantic drift: analyzing the raw data distribution is not the same as validating the model's ability to capture family trends, weakening the validation intent of FR-006.
